ads.author: Rebecca Gabel
ads.title: 1963 Corvette Cross County Bally Pinball WORKING, UNRESTORED
ads.description: 1963 Bally Cross Country Pinball Machine – Original & Working – Only 500 Produced Offered for sale is an exceptionally rare 1963 Bally Cross Country electro-mechanical pinball machine. Designed by the legendary Ted Zale, Cross Country was Bally’s second flipper game after returning to the flipper market in 1963 and is widely recognized as Ted Zale’s first original Bally design. With a documented production run of just 500 machines, it is one of the scarcest early Bally EM games and is seldom offered for sale. This machine is working and retains its original character, making it an outstanding addition to any serious Bally or early electro-mechanical collection. Features: * Manufactured by Bally in March 1963 * Original Ted Zale design * Only 500 produced * Electro-mechanical score reels * Original artwork and classic early Bally gameplay * Fully working Whether you’re a collector of early Bally machines, a Ted Zale enthusiast, or simply appreciate historically significant pinball machines, opportunities to acquire a Cross Country are few and far between. The machine presents well, plays as it should, and is ready to enjoy or display. Additional photos, gameplay video, and specific condition details are available upon request. Serious inquiries are welcome.

ads.author: Mike Mackey
ads.title: Nintendo HELIFIRE 1980 arcade cocktail game - ONLY 2017 Plays
ads.description: (Located near Lexington, KY) VERY RARE cocktail version of game only 2017 plays on coin counter. Nintendo HeliFire is rarely found in the wild—it’s the rarest of Nintendo’s early video arcade lineup. It came out after Radar Scope, both released in 1980, and prior to Donkey Kong, the game that made Nintendo famous. HeliFire had the “TUB-up” designation stamped on both the cabinet top plate and the monitor frame. This example has both, so it’s definitely a legit Nintendo build and not a conversion. And far rarer, than the already much sought after upright cabinet; because cocktail are produced in a much smaller percentage than uprights. Everything is original. Has switching power supply installed, but includes the original power supply as well. All boards have been serviced: socketed chips removed and legs cleaned, all tantalum caps replaced, nearly all electrolytic caps replaced. Monitor has been recapped. It has run for multiple hours at a time in attract mode and in play, both to burn in and to flush out any potential issues with the servicing. No problems have occurred. There are only 13 acknowledged owners of HeliFire in the VAPS (Video Arcade Preservation Society) census (combined uprights and cocktails). The VAPS numbers are a bit confusing because they only specify member activity from the past three years. Current VAPS stats list 3 uprights and 3 cocktails. Of course, there are likely more examples out in the wild, as not everyone records their serial numbers with VAPS. Walkthrough vid here: https://youtu.be/7-H8C4wyWbQ?si=xUoLG-4BUlTYWABm I will palletize for $30.
